item.title: India’s $2 Billion State Stake Sales Buck Equity Market Slowdown
item.description: India’s federal government raised $2 billion through stake sales in listed state-run companies last quarter, providing a rare bright spot for the country’s subdued capital market after the conflict in the Middle East slowed broader equity issuance.

item.title: Thailand Gets Serious About Chasing High-Spending Tourists
item.description: Thailand is done chasing tourist records. After decades of measuring success by ever-rising arrival numbers, the government is making its clearest push yet to prioritize how much visitors spend versus how many show up.

item.title: South Africa Plans to Tap Existing Rand Sukuk Bonds This Year
item.description: South Africa will tap existing rand-denominated sukuk bonds as part of its funding plans for the current fiscal year.

item.title: Japan’s FX Chief Says Past Yen Intervention Has Shown Impact
item.description: Foreign exchange intervention by Japan to support the yen two months ago was successful, and some US authorities voiced support, the nation’s top currency official said, as the yen hovers at a four-decade low.

item.title: OCBC to Raise Annual Tech Spending to More Than $771 Million
item.description: Oversea-Chinese Banking Corp. plans to increase its annual technology spending, including on artificial intelligence, to more than S$1 billion ($771 million), said a top executive.

item.title: An English Furniture Maker Faces AI Era of Bots Buying Sofas
item.description: With origins in the English countryside, The Cotswold Company is known for upscale furniture that evokes its bucolic backstory. Now it’s bracing for the era of artificial intelligence.

item.title: RBI’s Short Dollar Book Hit Record in May Before Policy Measures
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India’s short dollar book rose to a record $106.7 billion in May as the central bank used derivatives to defend the currency.

item.title: A Giant Cable Exposes Ireland’s AI Ambitions and Security Risks
item.description: A new Amazon transatlantic fiber-optic link is symbolic of the country’s tech economy, but also of its chronic lack of defense spending.

item.title: In Hindsight, Will a Coming Crash Seem Obvious?
item.description: The template for 2008 holds up after a midyear check on our imaginary hedge fund.

item.title: Global M&A Tops $2.5 Trillion After First-Half Deals Surge
item.description: The year was tipped to be a potential blockbuster in deals and the first half delivered, setting a pace that is likely to continue in the closing months of 2026.

item.title: Vertiv Opens Malaysia Plant to Meet AI Data Centers’ Power Needs
item.description: US data center equipment maker Vertiv Holdings Co. opened a factory in Malaysia, underscoring the rapid pace of AI infrastructure buildout in the Asia Pacific.

item.title: World Cup Across the Pond Moves More Pints in British Pubs
item.description: England fans have once again convinced themselves they’re heading to the World Cup final, despite possible match-ups with Brazil and Argentina ahead.

item.title: Perpetual Shares Surge as Company Reveals Potential Takeover Bid
item.description: Perpetual Ltd shares jumped before the company requested a pause in trading citing an approach over a potential takeover deal.

item.title: Aluminum Falls to Lowest Since February as Dollar Strengthens
item.description: Aluminum fell to the lowest since mid-February as a strengthening US dollar continued to pressure commodities.

item.title: Chinese Inverter Maker Sungrow Plunges 20% on Potential US Ban
item.description: Chinese inverter manufacturer Sungrow Power Supply Co. tumbled on Wednesday following a report that the US regulator is drafting a ban on imports due to national security concerns.

item.title: AI株乱高下の本質は目に見えない「供給力」－人手不足の日本を救う切り札とは
item.description: AIの真価や市場への影響はどう評価すればよいのか。需要と供給という視点を持てば、より理解しやすくなる。

item.title: Foreign Buying of Indian Bonds Hits Record After Tax Break
item.description: India’s bonds open to unrestricted global investment attracted a record monthly inflow after the government scrapped taxes on foreign investors.

item.title: Won Slides Toward Weakest Since 2009 as Global Funds Sell Stocks
item.description: South Korea’s won slid toward its weakest level since the global financial crisis, leading a retreat in Asian currencies, as the dollar strengthened and overseas investors sold local equities.

item.title: India Prop Traders Brace for RBI Funding Squeeze
item.description: Traders expect reduced returns from cash-futures arbitrage, options market making and index arbitrage.

item.title: Panasonic Targets Further AI Growth to Build on Record Valuation
item.description: Panasonic Holdings Corp., the Japanese electronics conglomerate, is attracting renewed investor attention as a beneficiary of the artificial-intelligence boom.

item.title: Mizuho Says Historic Slump in Yen Is Defying the Rates Rulebook
item.description: The slump in the yen to historic lows is forcing investors to rethink one of their most widely used strategies to gauge the currency’s moves, according to Mizuho Bank Ltd.

item.title: China Factory Activity Grows Less Than Expected, Survey Shows
item.description: China’s manufacturing activity grew less than expected in June, contrasting with an official gauge showing an uptick due in part to export strength.

item.title: Goldman Flags Up Oil Surplus Even as Nations Rebuild Stockpiles
item.description: The global oil market is set to swing back into oversupply as the impact of the Iran war fades and traffic through the Strait of Hormuz recovers, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.

item.title: Sixth Street, KKR Provide BridgeBio $1 Billion Preferred Equity
item.description: Sixth Street Partners and KKR & Co. have agreed to provide $1 billion of preferred equity to biotechnology company BridgeBio Pharma Inc., according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Yuan Rally Bets Fade as Options Traders Turn Bullish on Dollar
item.description: Bullish bets on the Chinese yuan are fading, with options traders unwinding one of the year’s most crowded trades after the Federal Reserve’s hawkish pivot.

item.title: Iran War Delivers Massive Swings in Chinese Petrochemicals Trade
item.description: The war in Iran has rewired China’s petrochemicals trade, allowing plants to ease their glut of the key building blocks for plastics, rubber and textiles by boosting exports.

item.title: Philippine Church Group Continues Protests, Disrupting Manila
item.description: An influential religious group continued its protest for a second day on Wednesday in support of a senator allied with Vice President Sara Duterte, again slowing traffic in Manila’s main highway.

item.title: Kotak Reclaims India ECM Lead With Mega Deals on Horizon
item.description: Kotak Mahindra Capital Co. reclaimed the top spot in India’s equity capital markets league table for the first half of the year, putting it in position to dominate a year with the country’s largest-ever initial public offerings in the pipeline.

item.title: Uber-Backed Lime, Investors Said to Price IPO at $25 Per Share
item.description: Neutron Holdings Inc., better known as Lime, priced its initial public offering at $25 per share, the midpoint of the marketed range, according to a person familiar with the matter.

item.title: Vimeo Parent Bending Spoons, Backers Raise $1.68 Billion in IPO
item.description: Bending Spoons SpA, which acquires struggling software businesses, and some of its backers raised $1.68 billion in a US initial public offering priced above a marketed range.
